channel.title: python on Luke Salamone's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in python on Luke Salamone's Blog

item.title: Python: Read & Write Json
Python: Formatting a string
item.description: There are three main ways to format strings in python: name = 'Luke' food = 'pizza' # old style "My name is %s and I like %s." % (name, food) # str.format() "My name is {0} and I like {1}.".format(name, food) # f-strings f"My name is {name} and I like {food}."

item.title: Managing Python Environments
item.description: Need to switch between python versions often? Use pyenv. Installing pyenv # install pyenv curl https://pyenv.run | bash # check pyenv install location which pyenv Install another python version # see a list of available python versions pyenv install --list # check installed python versions pyenv versions # installs python 3.7.5 pyenv install 3.7.5 Switch python versions # use python 3.7.5 everywhere on your machine pyenv global 3.

item.title: What is the Hardest Hangman Word?
item.description: It seems like a simple enough question. Which word should you choose so that it takes your opponent the most guesses to discover it? Should you choose a long word to use up your opponent’s guesses? Or perhaps a short word with obscure letters? In this document I look into this question. But first, a bit of background. If you’re not familiar with the rules of hangman, it is a guessing game played between two people.
